comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: `Contains | Same structure as .erk/impl-context/ plus run-info.json`
Reality: `.erk/impl-context/` only contains `plan.md` and `ref.json` (not `issue.json`, `progress.md`, or `README.md`). So `.impl/` contains `plan.md`, `ref.json`, plus `run-info.json` (remote only).

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/impl_context.py:65-83`

Suggested fix: Update both Contains rows to reflect actual file contents: `.erk/impl-context/` → `plan.md, ref.json`; `.impl/` → `plan.md, ref.json, plus run-info.json (remote)`

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Fixed: Updated Contains row for .impl/ to reflect actual files (plan.md, plan-ref.json, plus run-info.json) in 8c486b4

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-22 22:12 UTC_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: The `.erk/impl-context/` Contains row (line 21) says `plan.md, issue.json, progress.md, README.md`
Reality: `create_impl_context()` only creates `plan.md` and `ref.json`. No `issue.json`, `progress.md`, or `README.md` are written.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/impl_context.py:65-83`

Suggested fix: Update the Contains row to `plan.md, ref.json`

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: `create_impl_context()` calls `save_plan_ref()` to write `plan-ref.json`
Reality: `create_impl_context()` in `impl_context.py` writes `ref.json` directly (lines 72-83) — it does NOT call `save_plan_ref()`. The file is named `ref.json` (not `plan-ref.json`) when inside `.erk/impl-context/`.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/impl_context.py:72-83`

Suggested fix: Remove `create_impl_context()` from the 'Called by' list for `save_plan_ref()`, or add a note that `.erk/impl-context/` uses `ref.json` (written directly) while `.impl/` uses `plan-ref.json` (written by `save_plan_ref()`).

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Fixed: removed create_impl_context() from the callers list and added a note that it writes ref.json directly (not via save_plan_ref()). Fixed in e21549e.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-22 22:24 UTC_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: `ref.json` appears in the 'Files in Error' table as a file that causes Prettier failures
Reality: Two problems: (1) `.erk/impl-context/` is in `.prettierignore` (see root `.prettierignore:3`), so Prettier never checks any files in this folder. (2) `ref.json` is a JSON file, not markdown, so Prettier would not format it regardless.

Source: `.prettierignore:1-3`

Suggested fix: Either remove this row (Prettier doesn't check `.erk/impl-context/` files at all), or update the section to note that the Prettier cleanup detection pattern no longer applies after adding `.erk/impl-context/` to `.prettierignore`.

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Fixed in 2bd3fabaf: removed the ref.json row from the Prettier failures table. The .erk/impl-context/ directory is in .prettierignore so Prettier never checks it, and the row was inaccurate.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-22 15:39 PST_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: "Creates `.erk/impl-context/` folder with `issue.json` containing issue reference"
Reality: `impl_context.py`'s `create_impl_context()` creates `ref.json`, not `issue.json`. The legacy `issue.json` format is no longer created by this code path.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/impl_context.py:65-83`

Suggested fix: Change to "Creates `.erk/impl-context/` folder with `ref.json` containing plan reference"

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Fixed: Corrected issue.json reference to ref.json/plan-ref.json in 8c486b4

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-22 22:13 UTC_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: "Check for `.impl/issue.json` or `.erk/impl-context/issue.json`"
Reality: `get_closing_text.py` calls `validate_plan_linkage()` → `read_plan_ref()`, which reads `plan-ref.json` first, then `ref.json`, then falls back to legacy `issue.json`. The primary file is `plan-ref.json`, not `issue.json`.

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_closing_text.py:69-76`

Suggested fix: Change to "Check for `.impl/plan-ref.json` (or legacy `.impl/issue.json`) for plan reference"

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Fixed: Corrected issue.json references to plan-ref.json/ref.json with legacy fallback in 8c486b4

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-22 22:13 UTC_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate section context.

The surrounding section ("Local Worktree: `.impl/issue.json`") implies `issue.json` is the current format, but `erk plan submit` now creates `.erk/impl-context/ref.json` (not `issue.json`). The JSON block in this section documents the legacy fallback format only.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/impl_context.py:69-83` (creates `ref.json`)
Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/impl_folder.py:200-243` (`read_plan_ref()` tries plan-ref.json → ref.json → issue.json)

Suggested fix: Retitle section to 'Legacy Format: `.impl/issue.json`' and add a note that the primary format is now `plan-ref.json` / `ref.json`.

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Fixed in 2bd3fabaf: retitled section from "Local Worktree: .impl/issue.json" to "Legacy Format: .impl/issue.json" and added a note clarifying that the primary formats are now .erk/impl-context/ref.json and .impl/plan-ref.json.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-22 15:39 PST_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: `.erk/impl-context/` folder contains `plan.md`, `progress.md`, `plan-ref.json`, and `README.md`
Reality: `create_impl_context()` in `impl_context.py` only creates two files: `plan.md` (line 70) and `ref.json` (lines 73-83). No `progress.md`, `README.md`, or `plan-ref.json` are created.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/impl_context.py:68-83`

Suggested fix: Update the folder structure to:
```
.erk/impl-context/
├── plan.md    # Full plan content from issue
└── ref.json   # Plan reference metadata (provider, plan_id, url, etc.)
```
Also update the following JSON block label from `plan-ref.json` to `ref.json`.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Fixed: updated folder structure to show only plan.md and ref.json. Also corrected the JSON example from plan-ref.json to ref.json with empty labels. Fixed in e21549e.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-22 22:24 UTC_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — `.impl/` folder description in this doc (line ~27) says it 'Contains `plan.md`, `progress.md`, and optional `issue.json`' but `issue.json` is legacy (replaced by `plan-ref.json`). Since this file was touched, flagging for update.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/impl_folder.py:1-9` (module docstring lists `plan.md` and `plan-ref.json`)

Risk: Future agents may create `issue.json` instead of `plan-ref.json` based on this doc.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body: **Dignified Code Simplifier Review**: `datetime.now(UTC)` detected - use `context.time.now()` for testability instead. Direct `datetime.now()` makes tests non-deterministic. This violates the erk architecture pattern for time abstraction.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Fixed: Replaced datetime.now(UTC) with injected now_iso parameter for testability. Commit 8c486b4

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-22 22:13 UTC_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body: **Tripwires Review**: `datetime.now(UTC)` detected - violates architecture tripwire: "importing time module or calling time.sleep() or datetime.now()". Use `context.time.now()` for testability instead. Direct `datetime.now()` makes tests non-deterministic. See [Erk Architecture Patterns](https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/blob/master/docs/learned/architecture/erk-architecture.md).

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Fixed: Replaced datetime.now(UTC) with injected now_iso parameter. Callers in submit.py use ctx.time.now().isoformat(), exec script uses datetime.now(UTC) at CLI boundary. Commit 8c486b4

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-22 22:13 UTC_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body: **Dignified Code Simplifier Review**: Lines 213 and 189 have duplicate field validation logic - consider extracting to a constant. Example: `REQUIRED_REF_FIELDS = {'provider', 'plan_id', 'url', 'created_at', 'synced_at'}` followed by `if not REQUIRED_REF_FIELDS.issubset(data.keys()):` for cleaner reuse across the three JSON format fallbacks (plan-ref.json, ref.json, issue.json)

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Fixed: Extracted _REQUIRED_REF_FIELDS module constant and _parse_ref_json helper to deduplicate validation logic across plan-ref.json and ref.json parsing. Commit 8c486b4

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-22 22:13 UTC_

Fixed: Same as above — extracted _REQUIRED_REF_FIELDS constant and _parse_ref_json helper. Commit 8c486b4
